back_translation: The Lord spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying to them,
Speak to the sons of Israel, saying: This is the animal that you may eat from all the livestock that is on the earth.
Anything that has a divided hoof, divided into two parts of the hoof, and that chews the cud—among livestock—you may eat it.
But this you shall not eat from those that chew the cud and from those that have divided hooves: the camel, for it chews the cud but does not have a divided hoof; it is unclean for you.
And the rock badger, because it chews the cud but does not have a divided hoof; it is unclean for you.
And the hare, because it chews the cud but does not have a divided hoof; it is unclean for you.
And the pig, because it has a divided hoof and is split into two parts of the hoof, but does not chew the cud; it is unclean for you.
From their flesh you shall not eat, and their carcasses you shall not touch; they are unclean for you.
This you may eat from all that is in the water: anything that has fins and scales in the water, in the seas and in the streams—you may eat them.

And everything that does not have fins and scales in the seas and in the streams, from all the swarming life of the water and from all the living creatures that are in the water—they are detestable to you.
And they shall be detestable to you; from their flesh you shall not eat, and their carcasses you shall detest.
Anything that does not have fins and scales in the water—it is detestable to you.
And these you shall detest from the birds; they shall not be eaten because they are detestable: the eagle, and the bearded vulture, and the black vulture,
And the kite, and the falcon according to its kind.
Every raven according to its kind.
And the ostrich, and the screech owl, and the gull, and the hawk according to its kind.
And the little owl, and the cormorant, and the great owl.
And the white owl, and the desert owl, and the osprey.
And the stork, the heron according to its kind, and the hoopoe, and the bat.
Every swarming flying creature that walks on four is detestable to you.
But this you may eat from every swarming flying creature that walks on four, that has jointed legs above its feet to leap with them on the earth.
These from them you may eat: the locust according to its kind, and the bald-locust according to its kind, and the cricket according to its kind, and the grasshopper according to its kind.
And every swarming flying creature that has four legs is detestable to you.
And by these you shall become unclean: anyone who touches their carcass shall be unclean until evening.
And anyone who carries any of their carcasses shall wash his clothes and shall be unclean until evening.
For every animal that has a divided hoof but is not split, and that does not chew the cud—they are unclean for you; anyone who touches them shall be unclean.
And everything that walks on its paws, among all living creatures that walk on four, they are unclean for you; anyone who touches their carcass shall be unclean until evening.
And the one who carries their carcass shall wash his clothes and shall be unclean until evening; they are unclean for you.
And this is unclean for you among the swarming things that swarm on the earth: the mole, and the mouse, and the great lizard according to its kind.
And the gecko, and the monitor lizard, and the common lizard, and the skink, and the chameleon.
These are unclean for you among all the swarming things; anyone who touches them when they are dead shall be unclean until evening.

And anything upon which any of them falls in their death shall be unclean—from any vessel of wood, or clothing, or leather, or sackcloth—every vessel with which work is done—shall be brought into water, and shall be unclean until evening, and then shall be clean.
And every clay vessel into which any of them falls—everything in it shall be unclean, and you shall break it.
From all food that may be eaten, on which water comes, it shall be unclean; and every drink that may be drunk in any vessel shall be unclean.
And everything on which any of their carcasses falls shall be unclean; an oven and a cooking stove shall be torn down—they are unclean and shall be unclean to you.
But a spring and a cistern, a gathering of water, shall be clean; but one who touches their carcass shall be unclean.
And if any of their carcasses falls on any seed for sowing that is to be sown, it is clean.
And if water has been put on the seed, and any of their carcasses falls on it, it is unclean for you.
And if any animal that is for you for food dies, the one who touches its carcass shall be unclean until evening.
And the one who eats from its carcass shall wash his clothes and shall be unclean until evening; and the one who carries its carcass shall wash his clothes and shall be unclean until evening.
And every swarming creature that swarms on the earth is detestable; it shall not be eaten.

Everything that walks on its belly, and everything that walks on four, even all those with many feet, among all the swarming things that swarm on the earth—you shall not eat them, for they are detestable.
Do not make your souls detestable with any swarming creature that swarms, and do not make yourselves unclean by them, lest you become unclean by them.
For I am the Lord your God, and you shall consecrate yourselves and be holy, for I am holy. And do not make your souls unclean by any swarming creature that crawls on the earth.
For I am the Lord who brought you up from the land of Egypt to be your God; and you shall be holy, for I am holy.
This is the law of the livestock and the birds and every living creature that moves in the water and every creature that swarms on the earth.
To distinguish between the unclean and the clean, and between the animal that may be eaten and the animal that may not be eaten.
